name: Langdock
description: Enterprise AI workspace/assistant platform with an API over multiple underlying models

provider.comment: Berlin-based German GmbH; EU-native enterprise AI platform.

category.comment: Enterprise AI workspace/assistant product whose unified API is secondary to its chat/agents app; aggregates several vendors' models under one interface.

api-launched.comment: Company founded 2023 in Berlin; could not pin an exact public/GA month for the developer API from primary sources (checked 2026-07).

status.comment: Documented, generally available REST API with published rate limits and pricing.

auth-methods.comment: Bearer API key in the Authorization header; browser-origin requests are blocked (backend-only).

self-serve-signup.comment: API is a workspace-level enterprise product; API access has been obtained by contacting support@langdock.com. A 7-day self-serve workspace trial exists, but the API surface is oriented to enterprise/business workspaces rather than instant self-serve developer keys (checked 2026-07).

free-credits-trial.comment: 7-day free trial with EUR 5 AI-model credits and no credit card required; no permanent free tier.

regional-endpoints.comment: Region is specified in the request path (e.g. /openai/{region}/v1/... where region = eu or us); default hosting is EU (Azure).

data-residency-options.comment: EU data residency by default; models configurable to run within EU infrastructure, plus single-tenant, bring-your-own-cloud, and on-premise deployment options.

model-breadth.comment: Reaches four major model families through one API: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and Mistral (plus custom/BYOK models). Vendor diversity is limited to a handful, not a large aggregator catalog.

open-weight-models.comment: Mistral (open-weight) models are available through the unified API; custom OpenAI-compatible models can also be registered.

model-version-pinning.comment: Dated model snapshots are usable (docs show responses with e.g. gpt-4o-2024-05-13); the models endpoint lists available versions per workspace.

custom-fine-tuned-deployment.comment: Own OpenAI-compatible model endpoints can be registered as custom models (Langdock/custom-model-example) and called through the same API; BYOK is also supported.

model-catalog-size.comment: Product page cites 40+ models across OpenAI/Anthropic/Google/Mistral (checked 2026-07).

chat-messages-endpoint.comment: OpenAI-compatible chat completion endpoint with a messages array.

streaming.comment: Server-sent-events streaming via the stream parameter.

tool-function-calling.comment: Supported via tools and tool_choice parameters.

parallel-tool-calls.comment: The parallel_tool_calls parameter is explicitly listed as unsupported.

structured-output.comment: response_format {"type":"json_object"} (valid-JSON mode) is documented; server-enforced JSON Schema is not documented.

image-input-vision.comment: User messages accept image_url content parts for vision-capable models.

audio-input.comment: Completion endpoint content parts are limited to text and image_url; no audio input documented.

document-pdf-input.comment: The completion endpoint accepts only text and image_url content; documents/PDFs are handled via the separate Knowledge Folder API for RAG, not as a direct inference input.

image-generation.comment: No image-generation endpoint documented in the developer API (the API centers on completions, embeddings, agents, and knowledge).

text-to-speech-audio-output.comment: No speech/audio synthesis endpoint documented in the developer API.

embeddings-endpoint.comment: Dedicated Embedding API (OpenAI embeddings) for semantic search and RAG.

prompt-caching.comment: No prompt/prefix caching documented at the Langdock API level; underlying provider caching is not surfaced (checked 2026-07).

batch-api.comment: No asynchronous batch/offline endpoint documented; the completion API is synchronous.

max-context-window.comment: Largest window comes from the Google Gemini models offered on the platform (~1M tokens). This reflects the underlying model's context, not a Langdock-published figure; approximate, checked 2026-07.

extended-reasoning.comment: reasoning_effort parameter (none/minimal/low/medium/high/xhigh) exposed at the API level; not all values supported by all models.

built-in-web-search.comment: Web browsing exists for agents in-app, but a first-party web-search/grounding tool callable via the completion API is not documented (checked 2026-07).

built-in-code-execution.comment: A sandboxed JavaScript runtime exists for authoring integrations, but no model-facing first-party code-interpreter tool is exposed via the API.

mcp-support.comment: Native MCP: agents connect to remote/hosted MCP servers (STREAMABLE_HTTP/SSE, API key/OAuth 2.0), and Langdock also exposes its own MCP server endpoint so external clients can call Langdock agents. Configured at the agent/integration level.

files-retrieval-api.comment: Knowledge Folder API provides document upload plus managed retrieval/RAG.

agents-assistants-api.comment: Agent API for creating and managing custom AI agents above raw inference.

openai-compatible-api.comment: Completion endpoint follows the OpenAI Chat Completions specification; existing OpenAI SDK code works with a base-URL swap.

official-sdks.comment: No first-party client SDKs documented; integration is via the OpenAI-compatible endpoint (use the OpenAI SDK) or raw REST.

native-api-style.comment: Completion endpoints mirror each underlying vendor's shape (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral); the OpenAI-format endpoint is the headline. Agent/Knowledge/Embedding endpoints are proprietary REST.

published-sla.comment: Enterprise plans (1,000+ users, dedicated deployment) include dedicated support with SLAs; not offered on lower self-serve tiers.

rate-limit-model.comment: Default workspace-level caps (500 RPM / 60,000 TPM, per model), with increases available on request.

provider-fallback-routing.comment: Not a routing gateway; no automatic cross-provider/model failover documented.

status-page.comment: No public status/uptime page confirmed from primary sources (checked 2026-07).

no-training-default.comment: "Customer data is never used to train or improve AI models."

zero-data-retention.comment: Data retained only as necessary to provide the service and deletable on request; dedicated/on-premise and bring-your-own-cloud deployments give customers full data control (no external retention).

compliance-certifications.comment: SOC 2 Type II audited, ISO 27001 certified, GDPR aligned.

enterprise-sso-access-control.comment: SSO, SAML, and SCIM included from the Business plan upward, with user groups and permissions.

pricing-model.comment: Per-token usage-based pricing for the API product (model rate plus Langdock markup); the Chat & Agents workspace is a per-seat subscription.

unified-billing.comment: One unified API and account covers all reachable models/providers.

cache-batch-discount.comment: No cache or batch discount documented (no batch/caching surface on the API).

cheapest-entry.comment: After a 7-day free trial (EUR 5 credits, no card), API usage is per-token pay-as-you-go; no permanent free tier.
